core.when: When implementing API endpoint responses
core.action: Wrap each responses in {'result': ..., 'message': ...} structure

core.when: When reviewing rotki code and considering flagging error handling as missing or incomplete
core.action: assume error handling is missing without tracing function implementations to verify

consequence.description: False positive code review findings waste maintainer time and erode trust in review feedback. The codebase has utility functions like request_get() that handle errors internally, and flagging them as missing try-catch creates noise.

evidence_refs.summary: 2af250d CLAUDE.md:§No Assumptions Policy

guard_pattern.excuse: This code is missing a try-catch around the API call / the error handling looks incomplete / there's no validation
guard_pattern.rebuttal: Do NOT assume error handling is missing without tracing function implementations. Functions like request_get(), globaldb_get_*(), get_or_create_evm_asset(), and utilities in rotkehlchen.utils.network generally handle errors internally. ALWAYS trace function calls to their actual implementations before flagging issues.
red_flags: thinking 'this looks like it could fail'
suggesting try-catch without checking called functions
flagging KeyError/IndexError without examining try-except blocks
guard_pattern.violation_detector: Code review comment flags error handling as missing without citing the specific line and traced code path showing the gap

core.when: When considering adding VCR (cassette) tests to decoder tests
core.action: add @pytest.mark.vcr decorators to decoder tests claiming it makes tests more reliable

consequence.description: Adding VCR tests to decoder tests violates project policy. VCR tests record real API responses which can become stale, fail in CI, or capture non-deterministic data, defeating the purpose of automated testing.

evidence_refs.summary: 2af250d CLAUDE.md:§Backend Testing

guard_pattern.excuse: VCR tests will make the tests more reliable by recording real API responses / using VCR ensures deterministic test behavior / I'll add VCR to make sure external APIs don't affect test results
guard_pattern.rebuttal: Do not VCR tests. Let the human developers do it. The policy explicitly prohibits adding @pytest.mark.vcr to decoder tests. Let maintainers handle VCR test recording for decoder tests.
red_flags: thinking about using @pytest.mark.vcr
planning to record cassette files for API tests
wanting to make tests deterministic with recorded responses
guard_pattern.violation_detector: pytest decorator @pytest.mark.vcr found in decoder test file

core.when: When reviewing code and considering flagging ChainID or enum validation as missing
core.action: suggest ChainID validation or type validation when the type is an enum or pre-validated

consequence.description: ChainID is an Enum type with compile-time validation. Flagging it as needing runtime validation creates false positives that waste maintainer time and indicate lack of understanding of the project's type system.

evidence_refs.summary: 2af250d CLAUDE.md:§Pre-validated Types

guard_pattern.excuse: This ChainID should be validated at runtime / we need to check if the enum value is valid / the enum could have invalid values
guard_pattern.rebuttal: ChainID is an Enum type with compile-time validation. Do NOT suggest ChainID validation when it's an enum - the type system provides this guarantee.

suggesting runtime checks for enum types
recommending defensive validation for pre-validated types
guard_pattern.violation_detector: Code review comment suggests ChainID or enum validation without acknowledging the enum type provides compile-time guarantees

guard_pattern.excuse: This empty except block needs a comment explaining why it's empty / we should log errors here / let's add error handling

core.when: When working on code and considering skipping checksum validation for EVM addresses
core.action: use string_to_evm_address() or raw string literals instead of to_checksum_address() for EVM addresses

consequence.description: EVM addresses must be checksummed. Using string_to_evm_address() or raw lowercase addresses will result in database errors ('Invalid XXX account in DB') and potential loss of funds or incorrect transaction routing.

evidence_refs.summary: 2af250d CLAUDE.md:§Memories

guard_pattern.excuse: The address looks correct as lowercase / string_to_evm_address() should work fine / it's just a typing function anyway
guard_pattern.rebuttal: string_to_evm_address() is just a no-op typing function. It will NOT checksum the literal argument. Always use to_checksum_address() for EVM address literals. Wrong checksum causes 'Invalid XXX account in DB' errors.
red_flags: using lowercase hex strings for addresses
calling string_to_evm_address() on address literals
bypassing checksum validation
guard_pattern.violation_detector: EVM address string not matching checksum pattern 0x[0-9a-fA-F]{40} or using string_to_evm_address() instead of to_checksum_address()

core.when: When making typo corrections, rewordings, or trivial changes and considering creating a PR
core.action: submit PRs for simple typo fixes, rewordings, or AI-generated content that adds no functional value

consequence.description: PRs with only typo fixes, rewordings, or AI slop will be immediately rejected. Such attempts at starts or airdrop farming damage credibility and waste maintainer review time.

evidence_refs.summary: 2af250d CONTRIBUTING.md:§pull request guidelines

guard_pattern.excuse: This typo fix is helpful / small improvements add up / I'll submit this small change to help the project
guard_pattern.rebuttal: Do not make simple typo or rewordings or AI slop suggestions. They will be immediately rejected. The project explicitly states such attempts are considered starts/airdrop farming.
red_flags: planning to fix typos as a PR
making minor wording improvements
generating content that doesn't add functional value
guard_pattern.violation_detector: PR consists primarily of typo fixes, rewording, or minimal changes without functional purpose
